label: GET request
note: Fetch requires a second step to parse JSON. Axios unwraps it automatically.
fetch:
const res = await fetch('/api/users')
const data = await res.json()

label: POST with JSON body
note: Fetch needs manual headers and JSON.stringify. Axios handles both for you.

label: Error handling
note: Fetch only rejects on network failure — a 404 or 500 still resolves. Axios throws on any non-2xx status.
axios:
// throws automatically on 4xx / 5xx
const { data } = await axios.get('/api/users/99')

label: Base URL
note: Fetch has no built-in concept of a base URL. Axios instances let you set one once and reuse it.

label: Timeout
note: Fetch has no timeout option — you must wire up AbortController manually. Axios takes a single number.
